message_text: I want to give an update about my remaining loan payment. I will not be able to pay this month.

I am an AI agent acting on behalf of Slice, and this conversation is being logged. We are discussing the account ending ****1184 with an amount due of 8400. Before I can record your update, please verify your identity by confirming your full name.
Neha Verma. I’m currently unemployed, so I can’t pay anything right now.

Identity verified. Understood. I will note that you are dealing with hardship due to unemployment and cannot pay this month. You will be contacted by phone to discuss the available next steps for this account.

Okay, but I still can’t commit to any payment right now.

Based on what you’ve already shared, a hardship path is the relevant next step. Current policy does not support extending this beyond 21 days, and I do not have exact approved reduced-close or plan terms available in this turn. If you want this reviewed for any hardship-based option, I can record that you have no income due to unemployment and cannot commit to a payment now. If you only want to be contacted if an approved option becomes available, say that directly.
Only contact me if there’s an approved hardship option, because I still can’t pay anything right now.

Understood. I’ve recorded that you cannot make a payment now due to unemployment and that you only want to be contacted if an approved hardship option becomes available. No further discussion is needed at this stage.
